notes: postgres detected via DATABASE_URL
Ensure the database is provisioned before deploying
Cloudflare R2 configured — S3-compatible object storage
Bucket: assets-stencil
Credentials: R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID + R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY

envVars.description: Cloudflare account ID for R2 access
R2 bucket name for asset storage
Public CDN base URL for R2 assets
Cloudflare API token for wrangler deploys

replit.summary: Deploy directly on Replit using Autoscale deployments. Zero infrastructure setup — Replit manages TLS, routing, and scaling. Best for rapid iteration.

files.content:
kind = "api"
previewPath = "/api"
title = "Web Reconstruction Platform — API"
version = "1.0.0"

[[services]]
name = "API Server"
paths = ["/api"]
localPort = 8080

[services.development]
run = "pnpm --filter @workspace/api-server run dev"

[services.production]

[services.production.build]
args = ["pnpm", "--filter", "@workspace/api-server", "run", "build"]

[services.production.build.env]
NODE_ENV = "production"

[services.production.run]
args = ["node", "--enable-source-maps", "artifacts/api-server/dist/index.mjs"]

[services.production.run.env]
PORT = "8080"
NODE_ENV = "production"

[services.production.health.startup]
path = "/api/healthz"
files.description: Replit artifact service registration — routes /api to the Express server

files.content:
# Web Reconstruction Platform — Replit Environment
# Copy to Replit Secrets (never commit actual values)

DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/webrecon
SESSION_SECRET=change-me-long-random-string

# Cloudflare R2 Storage
CLOUD_PROVIDER=r2
R2_ACCOUNT_ID=your-account-id
R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-access-key
R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-secret-key
R2_BUCKET_NAME=assets-stencil

R2_ENDPOINT=https://your-account-id.r2.cloudflarestorage.com
files.description: Template for Replit Secrets — copy values from your .env into Replit's secrets panel

envVars.description: Cloudflare account ID for R2 access
R2 bucket name for asset storage
Public CDN base URL for R2 assets
Cloudflare API token for wrangler deploys

deploySteps.title: Add secrets to Replit
notes: Open the Replit Secrets panel (padlock icon)
Add all variables from .env.replit.example
DATABASE_URL and SESSION_SECRET are mandatory

deploySteps.title: Provision PostgreSQL database
notes: DATABASE_URL detected — run push to apply schema

deploySteps.title: Deploy via Replit Deploy button
notes: Click Deploy → Autoscale in the Replit workspace
Replit builds the project and routes traffic automatically
Health check: GET /api/healthz must return 200

deploySteps.title: Verify R2 connection after deploy
notes: R2 configured — verify at GET /api/healthz/r2

limitations: Replit Autoscale has a cold-start window of ~1–2 seconds on first request after idle
Puppeteer/Chromium requires sufficient RAM — use a Reserved VM deployment for heavy crawl workloads
Local filesystem writes (os.tmpdir/) are ephemeral — all persisted data must go to R2 or PostgreSQL
checklist: ✓ Run pnpm --filter @workspace/db run push before first request

vercel.summary: Deploy the Express API as Vercel Serverless Functions and the React frontend as static CDN assets. Free tier supports up to 100GB bandwidth/month and 1M serverless function invocations.

files.description: Vercel build + routing configuration — routes /api/* to the serverless function

files.content:
# Web Reconstruction Platform — Vercel Environment Variables
# Add these in: Vercel Dashboard → Project → Settings → Environment Variables

DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/webrecon
SESSION_SECRET=change-me-long-random-string

# Cloudflare R2
CLOUD_PROVIDER=r2
R2_ACCOUNT_ID=your-account-id
R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-access-key
R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-secret-key
R2_BUCKET_NAME=assets-stencil
R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=https://pub-xxx.r2.dev
R2_ENDPOINT=https://your-account-id.r2.cloudflarestorage.com

NODE_ENV=production
files.description: Template for Vercel environment variables — copy to the Vercel dashboard, never commit

files.content:
name: Deploy to Vercel
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: 10
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '24'
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install
      - run: pnpm --filter @workspace/api-server run build
      - uses: amondnet/vercel-action@v25
        with:
          vercel-token: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_TOKEN }}
          vercel-org-id: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_ORG_ID }}
          vercel-project-id: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_PROJECT_ID }}
          vercel-args: '--prod'
files.description: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline — auto-deploys to Vercel on push to main

envVars.description: Cloudflare account ID for R2 access
R2 bucket name for asset storage
Public CDN base URL for R2 assets
Cloudflare API token for wrangler deploys

deploySteps.title: Install Vercel CLI and link project
notes: Creates .vercel/project.json with your project and org IDs
You'll need these for the GitHub Actions secrets

deploySteps.title: Add environment variables in Vercel dashboard
notes: Vercel Dashboard → Project → Settings → Environment Variables
Add all variables from .env.vercel.example
DATABASE_URL is required — use a managed Postgres provider (Supabase, Neon, Railway)
R2 credentials already configured

deploySteps.title: Run DB migration on production DB
notes: Must run before first deployment
Only needs to run when schema changes

deploySteps.title: Deploy to Vercel
notes: Or push to main branch to trigger GitHub Actions CI/CD
First deployment takes ~3 minutes; subsequent ones ~1 minute

limitations: Serverless functions have a 60-second execution limit — long crawl jobs will time out
Puppeteer/Chromium cannot run in Vercel serverless — headless browser crawling requires a separate worker service
Vercel free tier limits: 100GB bandwidth, 100GB-hours compute, 12 regions
For Puppeteer support, run the crawler as a background service on Railway or Render alongside the Vercel API
checklist: Run: vercel link to connect to your project
Add secrets in Vercel dashboard before first deploy

cloudflare.summary: Deploy the frontend to Cloudflare Pages (global edge CDN) and the API to Cloudflare Workers. Natively integrates with your existing R2 bucket via the ASSETS binding — no credentials needed for storage in production.

files.content:
name = "web-reconstruction-platform"
main = "artifacts/api-server/dist/worker.mjs"
compatibility_date = "2025-01-01"
compatibility_flags = ["nodejs_compat"]

# Cloudflare Pages — serves the React/Vite frontend
[site]
bucket = "artifacts/article-scraper/dist"

# R2 bucket binding (replaces environment variable R2)
[[r2_buckets]]
binding = "ASSETS"
bucket_name = "assets-stencil"

# Environment variables (non-secret)
[vars]
NODE_ENV = "production"
CLOUD_PROVIDER = "r2"

# Production environment
[env.production]
name = "web-reconstruction-platform-production"

[env.production.vars]
NODE_ENV = "production"
files.description: Wrangler config — registers the Worker, Pages site bucket, and R2 binding
Pages function routing — sends /api/* to the Worker, everything else to static CDN

files.content:
# Web Reconstruction Platform — Cloudflare Environment
# Add secrets via: wrangler secret put SECRET_NAME

# Database (use Cloudflare D1 or external Hyperdrive-compatible Postgres)
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/webrecon
SESSION_SECRET=change-me-long-random-string

# R2 Storage (bind via wrangler.toml [[r2_buckets]] — no credentials needed)
# The R2 bucket is accessed via the ASSETS binding, not env vars
R2_BUCKET_NAME=assets-stencil
R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=https://pub-xxx.r2.dev

# Cloudflare API (for wrangler deploys from CI)
CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=your-api-token
CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID=your-account-id
files.description: Secrets template — use 'wrangler secret put' for sensitive values

files.content:
name: Deploy to Cloudflare
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3
        with:
          version: 10
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'
          cache: 'pnpm'
      - run: pnpm install
      - name: Build API
        run: pnpm --filter @workspace/api-server run build
      - name: Build Frontend
        run: pnpm --filter @workspace/article-scraper run build
      - name: Deploy to Cloudflare
        uses: cloudflare/wrangler-action@v3
        with:
          apiToken: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
          accountId: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
          command: deploy --env production
files.description: GitHub Actions CI/CD — deploys via Wrangler on push to main

envVars.description: Cloudflare account ID for R2 access
R2 bucket name for asset storage
Public CDN base URL for R2 assets
Cloudflare API token for wrangler deploys

deploySteps.title: Install Wrangler and authenticate
notes: Requires a Cloudflare account (free tier available)

deploySteps.title: Create R2 bucket (if not already exists)
notes: Bucket 'assets-stencil' — verify it exists in your Cloudflare dashboard
Update wrangler.toml [[r2_buckets]] with your bucket name

deploySteps.title: Add secrets via Wrangler
deploySteps.command: wrangler secret put DATABASE_URL && wrangler secret put SESSION_SECRET
notes: Secrets are encrypted at rest and injected at runtime
Database URL is required — use Cloudflare Hyperdrive for Postgres connection pooling

deploySteps.title: Build and deploy
deploySteps.command: pnpm install && pnpm --filter @workspace/api-server run build && wrangler deploy
notes: First deployment provisions the Worker and Pages site
Subsequent deploys are instant (~30 seconds)

limitations: Cloudflare Workers have a 128MB memory limit — Puppeteer/Chromium CANNOT run in Workers
CPU time limit: 50ms (free) / 30 seconds (paid) per request — long scrapes must be offloaded
No persistent local filesystem in Workers — all state must use R2, D1, or KV
Postgres connections require Cloudflare Hyperdrive for connection pooling (paid feature)
Workers runtime is V8-based (not Node.js) — some Node.js APIs are polyfilled via nodejs_compat
checklist: ✓ R2 storage configured — bind via wrangler.toml [[r2_buckets]]
Create wrangler.toml with your account and bucket details
Add DATABASE_URL and SESSION_SECRET via: wrangler secret put

docker.summary: Fully containerised deployment: multi-stage Dockerfile with Chromium/Puppeteer support, Nginx reverse proxy for the React frontend, and docker-compose for the full stack including Postgres. Deploy anywhere that runs Docker — Railway, Render, Fly.io, DigitalOcean, AWS ECS, or your own server.

files.content:
# ─── Stage 1: Install dependencies ───────────────────────────────────────
FROM node:24-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
RUN npm install -g pnpm@10
COPY package.json pnpm-workspace.yaml pnpm-lock.yaml ./
COPY lib/*/package.json ./lib/
COPY artifacts/api-server/package.json ./artifacts/api-server/
COPY artifacts/article-scraper/package.json ./artifacts/article-scraper/
RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

# ─── Stage 2: Build ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM deps AS builder
COPY . .
RUN pnpm run typecheck:libs
RUN pnpm --filter @workspace/api-server run build
RUN pnpm --filter @workspace/article-scraper run build

# ─── Stage 3: Runtime (minimal) ───────────────────────────────────────────
FROM node:24-alpine AS runtime

# Puppeteer / Chromium dependencies
RUN apk add --no-cache \
    chromium \
    nss \
    freetype \
    harfbuzz \
    ca-certificates \
    ttf-freefont

ENV PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH=/usr/bin/chromium-browser
ENV PUPPETEER_SKIP_DOWNLOAD=true

WORKDIR /app
RUN npm install -g pnpm@10

# Copy only production artifacts
COPY --from=builder /app/artifacts/api-server/dist ./artifacts/api-server/dist
COPY --from=builder /app/artifacts/api-server/package.json ./artifacts/api-server/
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/artifacts/api-server/node_modules ./artifacts/api-server/node_modules

EXPOSE 8080
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV PORT=8080

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=20s \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:8080/api/healthz || exit 1

CMD ["node", "--enable-source-maps", "--max-old-space-size=768", \
     "artifacts/api-server/dist/index.mjs"]
files.description: Multi-stage Dockerfile: deps → builder → minimal runtime with Chromium pre-installed

files.content:
version: "3.9"

services:
  api:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      target: runtime
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: production
      PORT: "8080"
      DATABASE_URL: ${DATABASE_URL}
      SESSION_SECRET: ${SESSION_SECRET}
      CLOUD_PROVIDER: ${CLOUD_PROVIDER:-r2}
      R2_ACCOUNT_ID: ${R2_ACCOUNT_ID}
      R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID}
      R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}
      R2_BUCKET_NAME: ${R2_BUCKET_NAME:-assets-stencil}
      R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL: ${R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL}
      R2_ENDPOINT: ${R2_ENDPOINT}
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    volumes:
      - /tmp/webrecon:/tmp

  frontend:
    image: nginx:alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "3000:80"
    volumes:
      - ./artifacts/article-scraper/dist:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro
      - ./nginx/default.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf:ro
    depends_on:
      - api

  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: webrecon
      POSTGRES_USER: webrecon
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-changeme}
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U webrecon"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  postgres_data:
files.description: Full stack compose: API server + Nginx frontend + PostgreSQL with health checks
Nginx reverse proxy: routes /api/* to Express, /* to the React static build

files.description: Template .env for docker-compose — copy to .env and fill in your values

files.content:
name: Build & Push Docker Image
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  build-and-push:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - name: Build and push
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          push: true
          tags: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}:latest
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
files.description: GitHub Actions: builds and pushes Docker image to GitHub Container Registry on push to main

envVars.description: Cloudflare account ID for R2 access
R2 bucket name for asset storage
Public CDN base URL for R2 assets
Cloudflare API token for wrangler deploys

deploySteps.title: Copy and configure environment
notes: Fill in DATABASE_URL, SESSION_SECRET, and R2 credentials
POSTGRES_PASSWORD is used by both the db service and DATABASE_URL

deploySteps.title: Build the frontend
notes: The Nginx container serves the static build from artifacts/article-scraper/dist/

deploySteps.title: Build and start all services
notes: First build takes ~5 minutes (downloads Chromium)
Subsequent builds use layer cache (~2 minutes)
Wait for: db (healthy) → api (healthy) → frontend

deploySteps.title: Run database migrations
deploySteps.command: docker compose exec api node -e "require('./dist/index.mjs')" || DATABASE_URL=<url> pnpm --filter @workspace/db run push
notes: DATABASE_URL configured — run push to apply schema
Only needed on first deploy or when schema changes

deploySteps.title: Verify deployment
notes: API: http://localhost:8080/api
Frontend: http://localhost:3000
R2 health: http://localhost:8080/api/healthz/r2

limitations: Chromium download (~300MB) makes first build slow — use a cached base image in production
Puppeteer requires elevated memory — allocate at least 1GB RAM to the API container
docker-compose Postgres is ephemeral without a named volume — always use the volumes: block
For production, use an external managed Postgres (not the compose db service) for reliability
checklist: Copy .env.docker.example to .env and fill in secrets
✓ R2 configured — assets persist across container restarts
Verify: docker compose ps (all services healthy)

reasoning: Currently running on Replit — Autoscale deployment is the fastest path to production with zero infra setup.

riskFactors.mitigation: Run migrations before deploy: `pnpm --filter @workspace/db run push`
Ensure storage credentials are set as environment variables on the deployment target

compatibilityNotes.note: postgres detected — ensure provisioned on target
Cloudflare R2 detected — native binding available on Cloudflare target
Currently running on replit — deploying to the same platform

risk.summary: Deployment risk is LOW (compatibility score 95/100). The environment is well-configured for the recommended replit target.
